description: Evaluates the AEM Cloud Service dispatcher config-authoring skill's ability to create, modify, and validate dispatcher configurations

evals.name: create-basic-filter-rule
evals.prompt: I need to add a filter rule to block direct access to /content/dam except for images and PDFs. Can you help me create the appropriate filter configuration for AEM Cloud Service?
evals.expected_output: A dispatcher filter configuration that denies /content/dam but allows /content/dam/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,pdf}
assertions: Output includes a valid dispatcher filter configuration section
Configuration includes a deny rule for /content/dam
Configuration includes allow rules for image and PDF extensions
Filter rules follow AEM Cloud Service best practices
No security vulnerabilities in the filter rules

evals.name: cloud-service-vhost-pattern
evals.prompt: Create a vhost configuration for www.example.com that follows AEM Cloud Service dispatcher patterns. Include the standard Cloud Service includes and make sure it works with the CDN.
evals.expected_output: VirtualHost configuration with Cloud Service-specific includes and CDN-compatible settings
assertions: Includes Cloud Service-specific Include directives (e.g., conf.d/available_vhosts)
Does NOT include legacy AMS-specific patterns
Follows immutable config pattern (no runtime modifications)

evals.name: cache-invalidation-cloud
evals.prompt: Our cache isn't invalidating properly when we publish pages in AEM Cloud Service. The statfileslevel is set to 0. Can you explain what's wrong and suggest a fix?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of statfileslevel behavior and Cloud Service-specific recommendations
assertions: Explanation mentions that statfileslevel 0 only invalidates exact matches
Recommendation suggests increasing statfileslevel to 2 or higher
Provides a concrete configuration example with /statfileslevel setting
Does NOT suggest AMS-specific troubleshooting (JMX, Felix Console)
